textByHashes.634b89360a33699e14ce2e2e576168c5a9a2fd73: Greetings, . I am here to test your wits with a simple riddle.
textByHashes.a75bf0b277078aeae90e9b2045149be79ad81333: Here is my riddle: I estimate there to be 1 million inhabitants in the world of Gielinor, creatures and people both.
textByHashes.86f0742e15cd20add3c8d85845f863b8477dd2db: What would be the number you would get if you multiply the number of fingers on everythings left hand, to the nearest million?
textByHashes.a4318bbe763f99d42f3d6b78519a3d9e451ee923: Here is my riddle: I have both a husband and daughter.
textByHashes.e926c60e552821e60a92cccb2b245b9ccf90466a: My husband is four times older than my daughter. In twenty years time, he will be twice as old as my daughter.
textByHashes.c8a1f597d0eab0de58782e2820be5edc3ef6aa57: How old is my daughter now?
textByHashes.b87cf92d71b994ae8b081979a35c9d4218ba6559: Here is my riddle: Which of the following statements is true?
textByHashes.ccb6d82773d19676ad79322970b563dda79dad22: Here is my riddle: Imagine that you have been captured by an enemy. You are to be killed, but in a moment of mercy, the enemy has allowed you to pick your own demise.
textByHashes.64f290cfaeb40ba0a3c8acb2d7d61ecc6259022d: Your first choice is to be drowned in a lake of acid.
textByHashes.8d3db4b9989b331177366ca2982010133bb17082: Your second choice is to be burned on a fire.
textByHashes.64bd45f211be6380949a886c1964135d44647cd3: Your third choice is to be thrown to a pack of wolves that have not been fed in over a month.
textByHashes.b3c67458ab21f1d33ae22cd288e11d5fea983b16: Your final choice of fate is to be thrown from the walls of a castle, many hundreds of feet high.
textByHashes.4859e2ea1c0dfe6852cb2ff99883b885a276eb77: Which fate would you be wise to choose?
textByHashes.2495281fb22f9db7ee97e88cdb7f31a624c1e216: Here is my riddle: I dropped four identical stones, into four identical buckets, each containing an identical amount of water.
textByHashes.cca7d2ecbab9bff048eefd1609fb9f3911f971e3: The first bucket's water was at 32 degrees Fahrenheit, the second was at 33 degrees, the third at 34 and the fourth was at 35 degrees.
textByHashes.d4a1fa5ce2bb842ed9a66bbb24e6bc9e760a9fc3: Which bucket's stone dropped to the bottom of the bucket last?
textByHashes.a6913347b02b30ec4f4f39c58123991fbf943459: Excellent work, . Please step through the portal to meet your next challenge.
textByHashes.f2fe43fdccc32b1b2ebd33169e29787bf0d0c514: No... I am very sorry. Apparently you are not up to the challenge. I will return you where you came from, better luck in the future.
